e4_content:
The next decade of marketing won't be won by whoever has the biggest audience. It'll be won by whoever has the deepest product understanding.

I ran a SaaS campaign last year that proved it. $8K across four creators. Three had 50K followers, one had 400. That 400-follower account drove 60% of our signups. Someone who'd been using the product for two years, understood it better than anyone we paid to promote it.

Does anyone actually have data showing follower count predicts conversion better than content quality? I've been looking and I can't find it.

@RallyOnChain is the first protocol built on the idea that it shouldn't have to. Campaign funds in on-chain escrow on GenLayer. AI scores submissions on six dimensions, all public before you write. I submitted to a campaign last week. 75,000 RLP in the pool. Score back in eight minutes. Follower count doesn't factor in.

Here's what I keep coming back to. If the most effective marketing is someone who genuinely understands your product, then how much of the current marketing industry is just noise? And when a protocol starts measuring this directly on-chain, what happens to the people selling the noise?
